headline: SAGA METALS CONFIRMS 21.6% TREO in Labrador Grab Samples as Wolverine Rare Earth Project Expands to 294 km²
snippet: SAGA Metals executed a title transfer agreement today to expand its Wolverine Heavy REE Project in central Labrador to 294.5 km², with certified lab results confirming rare earth enrichment in peralkaline pegmatites up to 21.6% TREO. The move follows SAGA's April 14 acquisition of the project and advances it toward a maiden NI 43-101 resource estimate.

headline: SILVER'S SIXTH STRAIGHT DEFICIT: World Silver Survey 2026 Warns of Continued Inventory Drawdown as Investment Demand Holds
snippet: The Silver Institute's World Silver Survey 2026, released April 15, projects a sixth consecutive annual market deficit as total supply eases modestly while investment demand remains resilient even at much higher prices. Silver averaged $40.03/oz in 2025 (+42% YoY), with intra-year highs above $121/oz recorded in early 2026.

headline: GOLD SURGES 2.8%, SILVER ERUPTS 5.38%: Mining Stocks Post Stellar Session as Dollar Index Collapses
snippet: Gold jumped 2.8% and silver surged 5.38% in one of the strongest single-session moves of 2026, with the SIL silver miners ETF up 6.56% and breaking above the key $38–$43 resistance zone. A weakening U.S. dollar and escalating trade tensions with China were the primary catalysts.

headline: COPPER BREAKS 2-MONTH HIGHS at $6.10/lb — US-Iran Ceasefire Optimism Erases Conflict-Driven Losses
snippet: Copper futures climbed to $6.10/lb, their highest level in over two months, fully recovering all losses since the start of the Middle East conflict as Washington and Tehran schedule a second round of ceasefire talks. Copper is up nearly 30% year-over-year, with tight Chilean supply and tariff risks keeping structural upside intact.

headline: CHINA SHOCKS RARE EARTH MARKET: Northern Rare Earth Group Hikes Q2 2026 Prices 44% to $5,390/Tonne
snippet: China Northern Rare Earth Group set its rare earth concentrate price for Q2 2026 at 38,804 yuan (~$5,390/tonne), a 44.6% jump over Q1. The hike reflects tightening Chinese supply policy and surging downstream magnet demand, adding fresh urgency for Western buyers working to diversify away from Chinese supply chains.

headline: K92 KAINANTU DELIVERS 46,743 oz AuEq in Q1 at 10.9 g/t — Record 3,007m Development Pace Targets Stage 3
snippet: K92 Mining produced 46,743 oz gold equivalent at 10.9 g/t AuEq from its Kainantu Mine in Papua New Guinea in Q1 2026, processing 37% more ore than Q1 2025. Record quarterly development of 3,007 metres surpassed Stage 3 expansion requirements; annual guidance of 190,000–225,000 oz AuEq was reaffirmed with production weighted to H2.

headline: ABRASILVER HITS 171 METRES AT 0.83 g/t GOLD in Diablillos Phase VI — Oculto East Step-Outs Keep Expanding
snippet: AbraSilver Resource reported broad gold-dominant intercepts from its Phase VI drill program at Diablillos, Argentina, including 171 metres at 0.83 g/t gold and 10.3 g/t silver from 230m downhole, with a 9m interval at 3.46 g/t gold. Results extend the Oculto East zone and reinforce resource expansion potential at the advanced-stage project.

headline: GOLD'S HISTORIC FLIGHT: April 2026 Surge to $4,800 Signals Growing Anxiety Over Fiscal and Monetary Stability
snippet: This opinion piece argues gold's sustained move above $4,800 is less a speculative bull run and more a distress signal — reflecting eroding confidence in fiscal deficits, dollar debasement, and central bank credibility. The author contends gold is now functioning as a barometer of systemic institutional risk, not merely an inflation hedge.

headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,800, SILVER EYES TRIPLE-DIGITS AGAIN: Asia Metals Morning Wrap for April 14
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian metals morning update covers gold consolidating above $4,800 after its recent surge, silver positioning for a potential return to triple digits, and copper firming amid geopolitical détente. The dollar index sitting at six-week lows provides a supportive backdrop for the metals complex heading into North American trade.

headline: CHINA IMPORTS 790+ TONNES OF SILVER in Early 2026 — Gold-Silver Ratio Flashes Bullish Signal for Miners
snippet: U.S. Global Investors' Gold SWOT analysis highlights China's outsized silver imports — over 790 tonnes in early 2026, driven by solar manufacturing — as a structural demand signal distinct from the Silver Institute's supply-deficit data. The piece also flags that the current gold-to-silver ratio has historically preceded strong outperformance by both silver and mining equities.
